name: Stability and Consistency Imply Convergence (515D)
statement_text: A stable and consistent general linear method is convergent.
context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
The preceding text analyzes error bounds for a general linear method, deriving a difference equation for $\eta_i$ that bounds the error $E[i]$. The proof uses stability and consistency assumptions to show convergence as $h \to 0$.

Variables:
\begin{itemize}
    \item $E$: error vector or error quantity at step $i$, $E[i]$
    \item $h$: step size
    \item $C$: stability constant
    \item $\alpha$: constant related to method coefficients
    \item $\beta$: constant related to truncation error
    \item $\eta_i$: bound on $E[i]$ defined recursively
\end{itemize}

variables.name: E
variables.definition: error vector or error quantity at step i, E[i]

preamble: The preceding text analyzes error bounds for a general linear method, deriving a difference equation for η_i that bounds the error E[i]. The proof uses stability and consistency assumptions to show convergence as h → 0.
